title: Mindfulness training improves working memory capacity and GRE performance while reducing mind wandering
headline: Mindfulness training improves working memory capacity and GRE performance while reducing mind wandering
summary: A randomized controlled trial of mindfulness / meditation examined focus & attention. Our results suggest that cultivating mindfulness is an effective and efficient technique for improving cognitive function, with wide-reaching consequences.
keyFindings: Our results suggest that cultivating mindfulness is an effective and efficient technique for improving cognitive function, with wide-reaching consequences.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.

The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice, so its findings don't transfer directly — though the shared directed-attention component may make some transfer plausible (see “Why this matters for the 7-minute practice” below).
simplifiedTitle: Mindfulness training improves working memory capacity and GRE performance while reducing mind wandering
aiSnippet: RCT, Psychological science (2013). Our results suggest that cultivating mindfulness is an effective and efficient technique for improving cognitive function, with wide-reaching consequences.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
